title: Delicate Smoky Egyptian Tomato Tagine
description: A light and yeasty Egyptian dish featuring tomatoes and smoke, made in a copper pot and suitable for a Whole30 diet. This recipe combines the flavors of Egypt with the simplicity of a tagine.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a copper pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the diced tomatoes, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Stir the ingredients together, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to simmer for an additional 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
